evidence_chain: Gold Box / FRUA item records store item pointer, name code 3, name code 2, name code 1 in the first four bytes.
Local ITEM0.DAX records have the same 16-byte shape and use nonzero values in bytes 1..3 as name-code triples.
Local START.EXE contains a contiguous Pascal-style item-name literal table from Knife through BLANK ITEM.
GAME.OVR indexes the local ITEMS side file with itemptr * 9 and reads detail bytes from 0x5370, 0x5372, 0x5373, 0x5374, 0x5375, and 0x5376 plus that product.

record_count_check.verdict: ITEMS cannot be a direct one-record-per-ITEM0-name table in the current local files.

items_text_check.verdict: ITEMS contains no printable strings long enough to be item names.

item0_name_records.name: Knife
item0_name_records.name_evidence: ITEM0 bytes [3,2,1] resolved through START.EXE item-name literal table
item0_name_records.detail_evidence: ITEMS is indexed 1-based by ITEM0 itemptr in GAME.OVR. Runtime references include 0x5370+itemptr*9, 0x5372+itemptr*9, 0x5373+itemptr*9, 0x5374+itemptr*9, 0x5375+itemptr*9, and 0x5376+itemptr*9.

items_detail_layout.alignment: 1-based by ITEM0 itemptr; file record N is used by ITEM0 itemptr N

proven_overlay_uses.use: type/category checks against values including 1, 7, 8, and 9
copied to runtime character/item field 0xdd and shown in item debug view
copied to runtime character/item field 0xdf and shown in item debug view
copied to runtime character/item field 0xe1 and shown in item debug view
used by item/combat checks; zero and 0xff are normalized to 1 in one routine
tested before dispatching a runtime item effect routine

candidate_fields.large_damage_candidate: record bytes 0,1,2 as dice/sides/bonus candidate; byte 0 also has non-damage type-code uses
candidate_fields.medium_damage_candidate: record bytes 4,5,3 as dice/sides/bonus candidate; matches weapon rows such as Knife 1d3 and Laser Pistol 1d8

item_index_like_fields.verdict: Some fields are in the ITEM0 index range, but this is weak evidence because zeros and small numeric fields are common in 9-byte binary records.

overall_verdict: Item names are decoded, and the ITEMS side-file blocker is narrowed: the table is now aligned as a 1-based itemptr detail table. Some detail semantics remain candidates, but non-name item fields no longer need to be treated as unaligned or untraceable.
